behaviour.description: General settings for everyday Windows behavior.
behaviour.title: Behaviour

openExplorerToThisPc.detailDescription: Changes File Explorer's default launch target. Instead of opening Home with recent files and favorites, it will open directly to the view that lists drives and network locations.
openExplorerToThisPc.name: Open File Explorer to This PC
openExplorerToThisPc.shortDescription: Open This PC instead of Home when File Explorer starts.

disableStartupDelay.detailDescription: Disables the Explorer startup serialization delay that Windows uses to stagger startup applications after sign-in. It sets both StartupDelayInMSec and WaitForIdleState to 0 in the current user profile so startup apps can launch almost immediately after login, including on Windows 11 builds that honor the extra idle-state gate.
disableStartupDelay.name: Disable Startup App Delay
disableStartupDelay.riskDescription: If you have many heavy startup apps, logging in may feel more bursty because more programs compete for CPU, disk, and memory at the same time.
disableStartupDelay.shortDescription: Lets startup apps launch immediately instead of waiting for Explorer's built-in delay.

disable8dot3NameCreation.detailDescription: Stops Windows from generating legacy DOS-style 8.3 short names for newly created files and folders. This reduces extra filesystem work in directories with many items, but only affects new items going forward; existing short names remain on disk. A restart is required before the change takes full effect.
disable8dot3NameCreation.name: Disable 8.3 Name Creation
disable8dot3NameCreation.shortDescription: Disables creation of DOS-style short file names for new files.

disableRecentItemsAndFrequentPlaces.detailDescription: Turns off Windows tracking for recent files and frequently used folders. After enabling it, recent and frequent items stop appearing in File Explorer Quick Access, the Start menu's recommendations, and the Run dialog history.
disableRecentItemsAndFrequentPlaces.name: Disable Recent Items and Frequent Places
disableRecentItemsAndFrequentPlaces.shortDescription: Disables recent file history and frequent folder tracking across Windows.

unlockLockScreenTimeoutSetting.detailDescription: Reveals the hidden power setting that controls how long the display stays on while Windows is locked. After enabling it, open Control Panel > Power Options > Change plan settings > Change advanced power settings and adjust Display > Console lock display off timeout to the number of minutes you want.
unlockLockScreenTimeoutSetting.name: Unlock Lock Screen Timeout Setting
unlockLockScreenTimeoutSetting.shortDescription: Shows the hidden lock screen display timeout setting in advanced power options.

appearance.description: Configure the visual details of Windows, from shell behavior to interface polish.

classicContextMenu.detailDescription: Disables the simplified Windows 11 context menu and restores the classic Windows 10 menu without requiring the Show more options step.

conflicts.windhawk: May conflict with any shell modification that restores the classic context menu by patching themes or system files.

classicContextMenu.name: Classic Context Menu
classicContextMenu.shortDescription: Restores the classic context menu layout in Windows 11.

fastTaskbarThumbnails.detailDescription: Reduces the delay before open app previews appear on hover. The interface feels more responsive.
fastTaskbarThumbnails.name: Fast Taskbar Thumbnails
fastTaskbarThumbnails.shortDescription: Speeds up how quickly taskbar window thumbnails appear.

fasterCursorBlinkRate.detailDescription: Adjusts how quickly the vertical text caret fades in and out inside text fields.
fasterCursorBlinkRate.name: Faster Cursor Blink Rate
fasterCursorBlinkRate.shortDescription: Increases the blink frequency of the text cursor.

hideGalleryNavigationPane.detailDescription: Completely hides the Gallery icon that was introduced in newer Windows 11 updates (Moment 4 and later).
hideGalleryNavigationPane.name: Hide "Gallery" in File Explorer
hideGalleryNavigationPane.shortDescription: Removes Gallery from the left side of File Explorer.

hideHomeNavigationPane.detailDescription: Disables the Home start page in the navigation pane. Useful if you prefer opening File Explorer with This PC instead.
hideHomeNavigationPane.name: Hide "Home" in File Explorer
hideHomeNavigationPane.shortDescription: Removes Home from the left side of File Explorer.

hideNetworkNavigationPane.detailDescription: Hides the system Network folder from the navigation pane. You can still access network resources through the address bar by entering \\computer_name.
hideNetworkNavigationPane.name: Hide "Network" in File Explorer
hideNetworkNavigationPane.shortDescription: Removes the Network icon from the left side of File Explorer.

removeShortcutArrows.detailDescription: Changes the system shortcut overlay icon to a transparent one.
removeShortcutArrows.name: Remove Shortcut Arrows
removeShortcutArrows.shortDescription: Removes the small arrows from shortcut icons on the desktop.

removeShortcutSuffix.detailDescription: Changes the system flag responsible for automatically renaming shortcuts.
removeShortcutSuffix.name: Remove Shortcut Suffix
removeShortcutSuffix.shortDescription: Disables adding the “ - Shortcut” text to the names of new shortcuts.

appearance.title: Appearance

tools.title: Tools
tools.description: Additional sections and system utilities of the app.

backup.title: Backups
backup.description: Create and restore snapshots of your tweak settings.
backup.applyConfirm: Apply this backup? Your current tweak settings will be overwritten.
backup.deleteConfirm: Delete this backup? This action cannot be undone.

network.description: Configure network adapter behavior, offloading, and TCP stack settings.
network.title: Network

enableBbr2.detailDescription: Switches all TCP supplemental templates (Internet, InternetCustom, Datacenter, DatacenterCustom, Compat) to BBR2. Internet, InternetCustom, Datacenter, and DatacenterCustom default to CUBIC; Compat defaults to NewReno. BBR2 measures actual available bandwidth instead of reacting to packet loss, which reduces bufferbloat and improves throughput on high-latency or lossy connections. LoopbackLargeMTU is disabled automatically because it is incompatible with BBR2.
enableBbr2.name: Enable BBR2 Congestion Control
enableBbr2.riskDescription: BBR2 may behave differently on some ISP networks or VPN tunnels. If you notice unstable connections after enabling, reset this tweak.
enableBbr2.shortDescription: Replaces default congestion providers with BBR2 across all TCP templates for lower latency and better throughput.

disableNcsiActiveProbing.detailDescription: Disables Windows NCSI active probing by setting both the main registry flag and the matching policy override. This stops periodic checks to Microsoft connectivity test endpoints such as msftconnecttest.com and dns.msftncsi.com. A full restart is recommended so Windows and shell components refresh their connectivity state.
disableNcsiActiveProbing.name: Disable NCSI Active Probing
disableNcsiActiveProbing.riskDescription: Windows may stop reliably showing the no-internet warning icon, captive portal detection can break, and some Microsoft apps or services may react worse to connectivity changes until you restore the default behavior.
disableNcsiActiveProbing.shortDescription: Stops Windows from periodically pinging Microsoft endpoints to detect internet access.

disableQosBandwidthLimit.detailDescription: Windows reserves a portion of your connection for system services by default. This tweak sets the QoS reservation to 0%, allowing browsers, games, and download managers to use 100% of your available bandwidth.
disableQosBandwidthLimit.name: Disable QoS Bandwidth Limit
disableQosBandwidthLimit.riskDescription: May slightly affect QoS-aware applications that rely on bandwidth reservation for prioritized traffic.
disableQosBandwidthLimit.shortDescription: Removes the 20% bandwidth reservation Windows holds back for system services.

enableNetworkOffloadingRss.detailDescription: Enables Receive Side Scaling (RSS) and TCP Offload, moving network packet processing from the CPU to the network adapter's own controller. This frees up CPU resources and can reduce latency in games or during heavy file transfers. A PC restart is required for the change to take full effect.
enableNetworkOffloadingRss.name: Enable Network Card Offloading (RSS)
enableNetworkOffloadingRss.riskDescription: Most effective on discrete network adapters (Intel, Realtek, Killer). On very old or cheap integrated adapters the effect is minimal, but the tweak is safe to enable regardless.
enableNetworkOffloadingRss.shortDescription: Offloads network data processing to the network card's own processor.

input.description: General settings for input devices and responsiveness.
input.title: Input

disableMouseAcceleration.detailDescription: Disables Windows mouse acceleration so cursor movement stays 1:1 with the physical movement of your mouse. This is especially useful for FPS games, aiming, and precise desktop work where you want consistent muscle memory instead of the pointer speeding up during faster swipes.
disableMouseAcceleration.name: Disable Mouse Acceleration
disableMouseAcceleration.riskDescription: If you are used to Windows cursor acceleration, the pointer may initially feel slower or require more desk movement, especially at lower DPI settings. If it becomes uncomfortable, reset the tweak and reboot Windows.
disableMouseAcceleration.shortDescription: Turns off Enhance Pointer Precision for a more consistent 1:1 mouse feel.

rawMouseThrottle.detailDescription: Windows already groups very high-rate mouse events before passing them through the system, usually at about 125 Hz. This tweak makes that grouping more aggressive by switching it to 50 Hz. Your mouse does not physically become 50 Hz, but Windows spends less time processing huge bursts of mouse events from 2000-8000 Hz devices. That can noticeably reduce CPU and DPC overhead and make FPS drops during fast mouse movement much less severe after a full reboot.
rawMouseThrottle.name: Reduce High Polling Rate Mouse Overhead
rawMouseThrottle.riskDescription: This is a tradeoff. Windows compresses mouse events more aggressively, so some users may feel slightly worse theoretical responsiveness or less precise motion in games that already behaved well. If mouse input feels worse after rebooting, reset the tweak and restart Windows.
rawMouseThrottle.shortDescription: Makes Windows compress high-rate mouse events more aggressively to reduce FPS drops and CPU load.

fastKeyboardRepeat.detailDescription: Changes how quickly a held key starts repeating and how fast repeated characters are produced after that. Faster presets make input feel more responsive in games, code editors, and general navigation, while slower presets stay more comfortable for everyday typing. After changing this setting, it is best to sign out and back in so Windows fully applies the new profile.
fastKeyboardRepeat.name: Fast Keyboard Repeat
fastKeyboardRepeat.riskDescription: Very aggressive repeat timing can feel too sensitive for normal typing, remote desktops, or applications that react poorly to rapid repeated input. If typing starts to feel erratic, switch back to a slower preset or restore the default profile.
fastKeyboardRepeat.shortDescription: Lets you choose a ready-made keyboard repeat profile, from the Windows default to faster and more responsive presets.

performance.description: General settings for system performance and responsiveness.
performance.title: Performance

configureKernelTimingChain.detailDescription: Changes how Windows handles system timing so games and other latency-sensitive apps can feel more consistent under load. On the right system this can reduce microstutter, smooth out frametime, and make input feel steadier after a full reboot.
configureKernelTimingChain.name: Configure Kernel Timing Chain
configureKernelTimingChain.riskDescription: This tweak does not help every PC. On some systems it can make frametime worse, introduce audio crackles, or add new stutter. If that happens, reset the tweak and reboot Windows.
configureKernelTimingChain.shortDescription: Can make games feel smoother and more consistent by using a lower-latency timing setup.

disableFaultTolerantHeap.detailDescription: Disables Windows Fault Tolerant Heap (FTH), a compatibility layer that Windows can automatically enable for apps that crash often. FTH tries to keep unstable apps alive, but that can reduce performance, increase memory overhead, and interfere with some games or native software.
disableFaultTolerantHeap.name: Disable Fault Tolerant Heap (FTH)
disableFaultTolerantHeap.riskDescription: Apps that truly depend on FTH for crash mitigation may become unstable again after you disable it. If a specific program starts crashing more often, reset this tweak and reboot Windows.
disableFaultTolerantHeap.shortDescription: Turns off the global FTH compatibility heap and clears existing FTH app assignments.

disableGameDvr.detailDescription: Disables GameDVR recording and related fullscreen capture hooks so Windows interferes less with games running in fullscreen. This can help if you want to keep built-in gameplay capture out of the way and prefer more predictable fullscreen behavior.
disableGameDvr.name: Disable GameDVR
disableGameDvr.riskDescription: Background gameplay recording and capture features that rely on GameDVR may stop working while this tweak is enabled. If you use built-in game clips or background capture, reset the tweak and reboot Windows.
disableGameDvr.shortDescription: Turns off GameDVR recording and related fullscreen capture hooks.

optimizeMmcss.detailDescription: Prioritizes games and low-latency audio workloads more aggressively so Windows spends less time favoring background activity. This can help reduce microstutter, improve frame pacing, and make gameplay feel more responsive on systems that feel slightly sluggish under load.
optimizeMmcss.name: Optimize MMCSS
optimizeMmcss.riskDescription: Background audio, video playback, streaming helpers, or other non-game multimedia tasks can become less stable while this profile is active. If you notice worse multitasking behavior, reset the tweak and reboot Windows.
optimizeMmcss.shortDescription: Can reduce microstutter and make games feel more responsive by favoring gaming and low-latency audio.

security.description: Adjust Windows safety prompts, downloaded file handling, and related system checks.
security.title: Security

disableAutoplay.detailDescription: Disables Windows AutoPlay and AutoRun for removable drives and USB devices by setting both NoDriveTypeAutoRun and the AutoPlay handlers override in the current user profile. This prevents removable media from automatically launching content or suggested actions when connected. A full PC restart is recommended so Explorer and shell services fully refresh the policy state.
disableAutoplay.name: Disable AutoPlay for Removable Media
disableAutoplay.shortDescription: Blocks removable drives and USB devices from automatically launching content or actions.

disableSecurityCenterNotifications.detailDescription: Hides the Windows Security shield tray icon and suppresses toast notifications about antivirus, firewall, and related protection checks. The policy key is created automatically if Windows has not created it yet.
disableSecurityCenterNotifications.name: Disable Security Center Notifications
disableSecurityCenterNotifications.shortDescription: Disables Windows Security notifications and hides its tray icon.

disableOpenFileWarning.detailDescription: Prevents adding Zone.Identifier metadata to newly downloaded files. This removes the “Are you sure you want to run this file?” prompt and the need to click Unblock in file properties.
disableOpenFileWarning.name: Disable Open File Warning
disableOpenFileWarning.shortDescription: Disables the security warning shown when launching downloaded files.

disableUserAccountControl.detailDescription: Sets User Account Control to “Never notify” and disables the limited LUA execution environment, removing all confirmation prompts when launching programs as an administrator.
disableUserAccountControl.name: Disable User Account Control (UAC)
disableUserAccountControl.riskDescription: Some third-party apps are overly strict about user safety and may limit part of their functionality when UAC is disabled. If you run into that situation, you can often work around it by launching the app with: runas /trustlevel:0x20000 "program.exe"
disableUserAccountControl.shortDescription: Completely disables UAC prompts and enforcement.

settings.description: Adjust application language, appearance mode, and window materials.
settings.installingUpdate: Downloading and installing the latest update...
settings.materialDescription: Choose the Windows backdrop material used for the app window. Mica and Tabbed require Windows 11, while acrylic is available on supported Windows 10 and 11 builds.
settings.materialUnavailable: The selected window material is unavailable on this Windows build. Falling back to no material.
settings.themeDescription: Choose between the clean light and deep dark application appearances.
settings.title: Settings
settings.updateChecksDescription: Automatically check for new app updates every 30 seconds.
settings.updateChecksDisabled: Automatic update checks have been disabled.
settings.updatePromptDescription: Winsentials {{version}} is available and ready to install.
settings.updatesDescription: Manage how the app checks for updates.

startup.title: Startup Apps
startup.description: Manage Windows startup entries across the registry, startup folders, and scheduled tasks.

empty.title: No startup entries
empty.description: No items match the current search and filters.

errors.partial: Some startup sources could not be read.
errors.enable: Failed to enable the startup entry.
errors.disable: Failed to disable the startup entry.
errors.delete: Failed to delete the startup entry.
errors.openFolder: Failed to open the containing folder.

states.loadingDescription: Sources are still loading. Entries will appear progressively.

networkStats.loadError: Failed to load network adapter information.

ram.title: Memory

storage.diskUnavailable: This disk is no longer available.
storage.title: Storage

errors.restartApp: Failed to restart the required app.

prompts.logout: A logout is required to apply this tweak.
prompts.logoutDescription: The change will fully take effect after signing out of the current Windows session and signing back in.
prompts.restartExplorer: Explorer needs to restart to apply this tweak.
prompts.restartPc: A full PC restart is required to apply this tweak.
prompts.restartPcDescription: The change will only take effect after a full Windows reboot.
prompts.restartService: Restart the {{serviceName}} service to apply this tweak.
